failure_surface: All code execution attempts produced SyntaxError at line 1, pointing to non-Python characters (tool wrapper markers) preceding the code block, preventing any spreadsheet manipulation from executing.
root_cause: The agent repeatedly wrapped executable Python code with function-call delimiters (<function=...> tags or special Unicode characters like <tool_call>) that the execution environment interpreted as part of the source code, causing immediate syntax failures before any spreadsheet operations could run.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Ensure that generated Python code for spreadsheet tasks is emitted as clean, standalone script without extraneous function-call wrapper tags or non-ASCII prefix characters that would be parsed by the Python interpreter.
evidence: All 4 execution attempts show identical SyntaxError: invalid syntax at Cell In[1], line 1 pointing to the <tool_call> character
Code blocks were prefixed with <function=Question solution generation> wrapper tags
The same syntactically-valid spreadsheet code was repeated 4 times yet never executed successfully
SyntaxError occurred before any pandas/openpyxl code could begin execution, indicating the failure is in code submission format, not spreadsheet logic

memory_items.title: Execute Clean Code Output
memory_items.lesson: Always emit executable Python code as plain text without surrounding function-call wrappers, special markers, or non-Python characters that precede the code content.
memory_items.generalization: When generating scripts for external execution environments, verify the first line of output contains only valid Python syntax. Any tool-specific formatting should be applied around—not within—the code block itself.

memory_items.title: Validate Execution Environment Compatibility
memory_items.lesson: Understand how the execution system parses and processes your output before assuming code correctness based on static analysis alone.
memory_items.generalization: Before relying on a solution, confirm that the output format matches what the runtime expects. Syntax errors at line 1 often indicate formatting issues rather than logic errors.

memory_items.title: Iterative Failure Diagnosis Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Repeated identical syntax errors suggest environmental or submission issues rather than code logic problems requiring recoding.
memory_items.generalization: When facing consistent SyntaxErrors across multiple submissions, shift debugging focus from algorithm implementation to output formatting, execution pipeline, and environment configuration.

edits.content:
## 7. Execution Hygiene
- **Clean Code Output:** Ensure all generated Python code is emitted without surrounding wrapper tags, markers, or non-Python characters that precede the code block.
- **Environment Validation:** Verify the execution system's expected output format before submitting solutions. SyntaxErrors at line 1 typically indicate submission-format issues.
- **Failure Pattern Recognition:** When encountering identical errors across multiple attempts, examine output formatting and execution pipeline before modifying code logic.
